Also prohibits hosting official Chinese visitors at NASA facilities. Exceptions allowed if NASA/OSTP/NSC certify (after FBI consultation) that activities pose no technology transfer risk and do not involve officials with direct involvement in human rights violations, with 30-day advance notification to committees.

provisions.description: Notwithstanding any other provision of law or treaty, prohibits use of any funds to pay administrative expenses or compensate any officer or employee in connection with requiring an export license for export to Canada of components, parts, accessories or attachments for ITAR Category I firearms with total value not exceeding $500 wholesale, subject to specified conditions. Provides exceptions for fully automatic firearms, certain firearm components, and re-export to third countries. Allows the President to temporarily require export licenses if Canada maintains inadequate import controls.

provisions.description: Extends temporary judgeships by amending three public laws: (1) Section 203(c) of P.L. 101-650 for the District of Kansas (by 1 year) and District of Hawaii (by 1 year); (2) Section 406 of P.L. 109-115 for the Eastern District of Missouri (by 1 year); and (3) Section 312(c)(2) of P.L. 107-273 for three districts including Central District of California and Western District of North Carolina (by 1 year each).

notes: Subsection (a) extends District of Kansas temporary judgeship from 28.5 to 29.5 years and District of Hawaii from 25.5 to 26.5 years
Subsection (b) extends Eastern District of Missouri temporary judgeship from 26.5 to 27.5 years
Subsection (c) extends three temporary judgeships: general from 17 to 18 years, Central District of California from 16.5 to 17.5 years, Western District of North Carolina from 15 to 16 years

provisions.description: Establishes an Immigration Detention Ombudsman within the Department of Homeland Security by amending Subtitle A of Title IV of the Homeland Security Act of 2002, adding a new Section 405. The Ombudsman reports directly to the Secretary and has functions including investigating complaints, conducting unannounced inspections, and providing redress for misconduct in immigration detention facilities.
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text:
ral
Subtitle A of title IV of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 is amended by adding the following new section: 

SEC. 405. 
Ombudsman for Immigration
notes: Amends the Homeland Security Act of 2002 to add Section 405 creating the Ombudsman for Immigration Detention
Ombudsman has unfettered access to detention facilities and detainee records
ICE Director and CBP Commissioner must respond to Ombudsman recommendations within 60 days
Annual report to Congress required

cross_references.target: Subtitle A of Title IV of the Homeland Security Act of 2002
cross_references.description: Adds new Section 405 establishing Ombudsman for Immigration Detention
